title: React Component Lifecycle – Mounting, Updating & Unmounting

meta.title: React Lifecycle Guide – Mastering the Component Journey (2026)
meta.description: Understand the three phases of the React component lifecycle: Mounting, Updating, and Unmounting. Learn how to use lifecycle methods and the useEffect hook.

content.value: Every React component goes through a lifecycle of three phases: Mounting (creation), Updating (growth), and Unmounting (deletion). In modern React, we primarily manage these phases using the `useEffect` hook in functional components.

This happens when a component is created and inserted into the DOM. In Class components, `componentDidMount` is used here. In Functional components, it's represented by a `useEffect` with an empty dependency array.

Updating occurs when a component's state or props change. React re-renders the component to reflect these changes. You can trigger logic specifically when certain values change by adding them to the dependency array.
This is the final phase when a component is removed from the DOM. It is critical for cleanup to prevent memory leaks, such as clearing intervals or unsubscribing from services.

value: **Avoid side effects in render**: Never update state directly in the function body.
**Always clean up**: Cancel API requests or subscriptions in the return function of useEffect.
**Dependency Array Accuracy**: Ensure all variables used inside useEffect are listed in the dependency array.
**Use multiple Effects**: Break up unrelated logic into separate `useEffect` calls for better readability.

faq.question: Does useEffect run on every render?
faq.answer: If you don't provide a dependency array, yes. If you provide an empty array `[]`, it runs only once on mount.

faq.question: What is the 'Cleanup Function'?
faq.answer: It's the function returned by `useEffect`. It runs before the component unmounts and before the effect re-runs to clean up previous logic.

faq.question: Can I use async functions directly in useEffect?
faq.answer: No. You must define an async function inside the effect and call it, because `useEffect` expects a cleanup function or nothing as a return value, not a Promise.

quiz.question: Which hook is used to handle lifecycle events in functional components?
How do you run an effect only once on mount?
When does the cleanup function in useEffect run?
options: Before unmounting and before re-running the effect
